data.title: Industrial control system loop check
data.solution:
zero = int(input())
span = int(input())
mv = float(input())

output = (((mv - zero) / (span - zero)) * 16) + 4
print(f"{output:.2f} mA")
data.statement: An instrument technician will be expected to know the current in a control loop if they are given  the range details, along with the reading of the measured variable. You must determine what the input current to the control system is for each circuit, in milliamps (mA). The lower range will give a 4 mA output and higher range will give 20 mA. The control systems engineer requires you to calculate the loop current for each circuit as soon as possible.

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

An instrument technician will be expected to know the current in a control loop if they are given the range details, along with the reading of the measured variable. You must determine what the input current to the control system is for each circuit, in milliamps (mA). The lower range will give a 4 mA output and higher range will give 20 mA. The control systems engineer requires you to calculate the loop current for each circuit as soon as possible.
Input
Line 1: An integer zero is the lower range limit.
Line 2: An integer span is the higher range limit.
Line 3: A float mv is the measured variable
Please note that the zero point may be zero, negative or elevated.
Output
The float output will be between 4 mA and 20 mA and must be given to 2 decimal places.
Constraints
-1000 <= zero <= 1000
1 <= span <=10000
-1000 <= mv <=10000
4.00 mA <= output <= 20.00 mA
Example
Input
0
10
5
Output
12.00 mA
